root_cause.bug1_websocket_disconnect: ws.rs send_task used `while let Ok(event) = event_rx.recv().await` which exits on RecvError::Lagged (broadcast buffer overflow, 256 events). A burst of agent events exceeding the buffer caused the WS send_task to terminate, which triggered the select! in handle_socket to return, permanently closing the WebSocket connection. The fix replaces the pattern-match loop with an explicit match on RecvError variants: Lagged sends a {"type":"lag","skipped":N} notification to the client and continues; Closed (server shutdown) exits cleanly. The WS connection now survives across multiple agent runs and event bursts.
root_cause.bug2_retry_404: POST /api/agent/retry is correctly registered and the handler exists. The HTTP 404 was emitted by the handler itself (StatusCode::NOT_FOUND) when session_manager.get(last_session_id) returned None — meaning the session UUID was stored in last_session_id but the session was not in the SQLite DB. Root cause: both submit_goal and run_agent_from_history called session_manager.save() with `let _ = ...`, silently discarding save errors, then unconditionally set last_session_id. A transient DB write failure would leave last_session_id pointing to a non-existent session, causing all subsequent retry/regenerate/edit-resend calls to return 404. The fix: wrap save() in a proper error match that logs the failure at ERROR level. last_session_id is still set even on save failure (since the session object is valid and a re-save on retry would succeed), and the save error is now visible in logs for diagnosis.

changes.ws.rs: Replaced `while let Ok(event) = event_rx.recv().await` with an explicit loop + match on RecvError::Lagged (continue with lag notification) and RecvError::Closed (break). Added `use tokio::sync::broadcast::error::RecvError`. Updated doc comment to explain multi-run survival.
changes.api.rs: Fixed two silent `let _ = session_manager.save()` calls in submit_goal (line ~259) and run_agent_from_history (line ~632). Both now use explicit match/if-let that log save errors at ERROR level while still setting last_session_id so the session ID is available for retry.
